g.data-narration: Stack is a LIFO data structure fundamental to computer science applications
Push adds to top, Pop removes from top - the LIFO property enables many applications
g.text: Stack follows Last-In, First-Out: last element added is first removed

g.data-narration: Stacks enable expression evaluation, manage function calls, solve mazes, and implement undo features
Postfix notation eliminates parentheses and precedence rules, ideal for stack-based evaluation
g.text: Compare infix, prefix, and postfix notations for expression A + B * C

g.data-narration: The algorithm uses a stack to reorder operators based on precedence, outputting operands immediately

g.text: 4. If operator → pop higher/equal precedence, then push
5. End of expression → pop all operators

g.data-narration: Walking through (A+B)*C: parentheses group first, then operators based on precedence
g.text: Example: Convert (A + B) * C to Postfix

g.data-narration: Postfix evaluation is straightforward: scan left-to-right, push operands, apply operators to top two stack elements
The call stack manages function execution: each call pushes a frame, each return pops it
g.text: When functions call each other, stack frames store return address, parameters, and local variables

g.data-narration: Backtracking uses a stack to remember exploration paths, enabling return to previous decision points
g.text: At dead end → Pop() to backtrack
3. Dead end? Pop and try another

g.data-narration: DFS explores graph depth-first using a stack: push unvisited neighbors to explore deeper paths first
g.text: A → B → D → C (visits deep first)
Uses LIFO to go deep before backtracking

g.data-narration: Two stacks implement undo/redo: undo stores actions, redo stores reversed actions for re-execution
Browser history uses two stacks: back stack for visited pages, forward stack for pages after going back
g.text: Back: push to forward, navigate to new top
Forward: push to back, navigate to new top

g.data-narration: Stacks are fundamental to understanding recursion, compiler design, and algorithm optimization
g.text: LIFO property enables: Expression Evaluation • Function Calls • Backtracking • DFS • Undo/Redo • Browser History
Stacks are fundamental to understanding recursion, compiler design, and algorithm optimization
